title: In Vivo Metabolomics
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"eavfj","text":"This protocol describes steps for targeting metabolites with [U-13C]glucose in mice for metabolomics analysis.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Materials Surgical tools – scissors, tweezers, forceps, spatula, razor Heating pad (K&H Thermo-Peep Heated Pad) Tail illuminator (Braintree Scientific: MTI STD) Glue (Loctite 1699233) 30g needles (BD: 305106) PE tubing (BD: PE#10) Syringe pump (WPI: SP220I) 1mL syringes (BD: 309628) Vapor splitter (VetEquip: CX-IM 5 place manifold) Dry ice Sterile 1X PBS or saline [U-13C]glucose solutions (Cambridge Isotope Laboratories: CLM-1396-1) Glucometer (Zoetis: AlphaTrak 2) Luer adapter
Day of Procedure
Protocol adapted from UCLA Metabolomics Center. Materials Aluminum foil Metal Pad Dry ice Hammer Microcentrifuge tubes 80% methanol Vortex mixer Ice Centrifuge Screw-cap glass vials
Day Prior to Procedure
Weigh mice.
Fast mice overnight (14-16 hours).
Make [U-13C]glucose solutions with PBS/saline. Bolus: 200 µL of 0.4 mg/g. Infusion: 75 µL of 0.72 mg/g.
Anesthetize mice in an induction chamber with 2% isoflurane with flowrate set to 1.2 L/min.
Move a mouse to the tail illuminator, cannulate the tail vein and glue needle in place.
Test that the catheter works by infusing 0.1 mL of saline – the saline should flow up the tail vein if it was cannulated correctly.
Gently move mouse and catheter to the isoflurane-splitter.
Repeat steps 3-4 to fill up the remaining spaces on the isoflurane-splitter (up to 5 mice).
Prick the paw of each mouse with a needle (18G) to measure the pre-infusion blood glucose level.
Inject the bolus glucose solution into each mouse and transfer the luer adapter to the infusion syringes.
Start infusion at a flow rate of 150 µL/h for 75 µL (30 min).
After infusion is done, euthanize mice by cervical dislocation.
Quickly dissect out the brain and flash freeze in isopentane cooled to -80C or liquid nitrogen.
Store samples in -80°C until further processing.
Wrap frozen brains in pre-cooled aluminum foil and place on a metal pad on ground dry ice.
Pulverize tissue with a hammer.
Transfer the ground tissue into a pre-cooled microcentrifuge tube on dry ice.
Add 1mL 80% MeOH at -80°C and vortex vigorously for 30 s.
Incubate samples at -80°C for an hour.
Allow samples to warm up a bit on ice.
Vortex samples again for 30 s.
Centrifuge at top speed (16,000 g) for 15 min at 4°C.
Transfer the supernatant to a screw-cap glass vial and store at -80°C until shipping to UCLA Metabolomics Core.

Protocol from UCLA Metabolomics Center. Materials Q Exactive mass spectrometer (Thermo Scientific) Vanquish Flex UHPLC (Thermo Scientific) Ion Chromatography System ICS-5000+ Luna NH2 3um 100A (150 × 2.0 mm) column (Phenomenex) LC-MS grade acetonitrile (ACN) LC-MS grade water 5 M ammonium acetate ammonium hydroxide
Dilute the MeOH extract with 50% ACN to 50 mg tissue/ml concentration (1:5-1:10).
Spin 10 min at full speed at 4C to clarify the samples.
Transfer the supernatant to glass vials with glass insert.
Combine 10 ul of each sample to make a pool sample used to condition the column prior loading the individual samples.
Place samples in autosampler 4C.
Load 5 ul onto a Luna NH2 column using a Vanquish Flex.

Separate the metabolites at a flow rate of 200 μl/min with mobile phases A (5 mM NH4AcO pH 9.9) and B (100% ACN) using a linear gradient from 15% A to 95% A over 18 min followed by a 7 min isocratic wash at 95% A.
Re-equilibrate the column to 15% A for 15 min prior to loading the next sample.
Acquire data with a Q Exactive mass spectrometer using polarity switching in full scan mode using a range of 70-975 m/z and 70.000 resolution.
Convert the RAW files to mzXML format using the msConverter (part of open source ProteoWizard).

Extract metabolite intensities with Maven (v 8.1.27.11) using a targeted list of polar metabolites of central carbon metabolism with expected retention time and accurate mass measurements (< 5 ppm) for identification.
Perform data analysis using R scripts functions (or other tools). Correct isotopologue data for the natural abundance of C13 using AccuCor.
creator: Haru Yamamoto
